name: The Inner Sanctum
subtitle: Deeper into darkness, closer to truth
location: Valdris's Inner Sanctum and Core Chamber

scenes.narration: The deeper passages of the fortress are not halls — they are arteries. The stone pulses with something that might be blood, might be power. The air tastes of fear and ancient memory.
Lenneth walks ahead, her sword blazing with divine light. The darkness recoils from her presence.

dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
dialogue.text: I have walked between worlds for millennia. I have never felt a darkness like this. It is not evil — it is grief. Infinite, absolute, all-consuming grief.

dialogue.speaker: Leon
dialogue.text: A being that became everything it feared. That is a tragedy I understand all too well.

dialogue.speaker: Rydia
dialogue.text: The eidolons weep. They sense what he was — and what he refused to be.

dialogue.speaker: Kain
dialogue.text: Then we show him another path. We show him that what he fears can be faced.

chapters.title: The Fortress Ramparts
chapters.map_hint: Fight through the Fortress Ramparts — the Shadow Titan waits at the inner core.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The fortress ramparts pulse like arteries — corrupted creatures emerging from every shadow, things that were once guardians now twisted beyond recognition.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
pre_dialogue.text: They were sentient once. Let us free them from this torment.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
pre_dialogue.text: I have walked between worlds for millennia. This darkness is not evil — it is grief. Infinite grief. We cut through it.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Kain
pre_dialogue.text: Then we show it another path. We show it that what it fears can be faced.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: At the core of the fortress, a scholar's chamber — ancient texts, constellations mapped on walls. And in the center, a mirror.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: He was a scholar once. Curious about death. And when he could not escape it, he tried to escape life instead.

post_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
post_dialogue.text: Then the darkness rises. Not Valdris himself — something he became. Vast. Immense. A titan of shadow.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: This is not him. This is what he became when he refused to let go. We break it. And free what remains.

pre_dialogue.speaker: narrator
pre_dialogue.text: The darkness coalesces into something immense — a titan of shadow and malice, wearing Valdris's form like armor.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
pre_dialogue.text: This is not him. This is what he became when he refused to let go.

pre_dialogue.speaker: Xiao
pre_dialogue.text: Then we break it. And we free what remains.

post_dialogue.speaker: narrator
post_dialogue.text: The titan staggers, cracks appearing in its form. But it does not fall. It simply withdraws, pulling back deeper into the fortress.

post_dialogue.speaker: Hutao
post_dialogue.text: It is weakening. We wounded something that thought itself invulnerable.

post_dialogue.speaker: Nilou
post_dialogue.text: We are close. One more push — one final confrontation.

post_dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
post_dialogue.text: Then we finish this. All of us. Together.

scenes.narration: As the Shadow Titan withdraws, a figure steps forward from the gathered light — a king in golden armor, his eyes holding the weight of two hundred years.

dialogue.speaker: Leon
dialogue.text: I have ruled for ages. Commanded armies. Built monuments meant to last forever. I chose immortality, thinking it was strength.

dialogue.speaker: Leon
dialogue.text: I see now that true immortality is purpose. And my purpose — here, now — is to stand with you, six warriors against the darkness.

dialogue.speaker: Ayaka
dialogue.text: You were a king. Now you are something greater — you are free.

dialogue.speaker: Lenneth
dialogue.text: Seven now. The number of completion. The number of final judgment.
